isolation.testingApproach: Direct Python testing (import and call Database methods)

assertions.title: Database class exists and is importable

steps.observed: Import succeeded: from kiro.core.database import Database

steps.action: Verify Database is a class type
steps.observed: Database is a class type (class name: Database, module: kiro.core.database)
Database instance created with db_path=':memory:' and timeout=5.0

assertions.title: Database class provides context manager support

steps.observed: Entered context manager successfully, connection established

steps.action: Exit context and verify connection closed
steps.expected: Connection is closed after exiting context
steps.observed: Connection properly closed after context exit (attempting to use closed connection raised exception as expected)

assertions.title: Database class handles connection errors gracefully

steps.action: Create Database instance with invalid path

steps.expected: Raises sqlite3.Error with clear error message
steps.observed: sqlite3.Error raised with message: 'unable to open database file'
Error logged: 'Failed to connect to database at \nonexistent\directory\that\does\not\exist\database.db: unable to open database file'

summary: Tested 4 assertions: all 4 passed. Database class basic functionality is working correctly - class is importable, provides connection management with _connect() method, supports context manager pattern with proper cleanup, and handles connection errors gracefully with clear error messages.
